title: Charles V Establishes a Royal Library at the Louvre
highlights: In 1368 King Charles V converted the fortress of the Louvre into a royal palace, and established a royal library there. This library was the origin of the Bibliothèque nationale de France. "Charles had received a collection of manuscripts from his predecessor, John II, and transferred them to the Louvre from the Palais de la Cité.
